scope: This subject imparts knowledge on stereo-chemical aspects of organic compounds and organic reactions, important named reactions, chemistry of important heterocyclic compounds. It also emphasizes on medicinal and other uses of organic compounds.
objectives: understand the methods of preparation and properties of organic compounds
explain the stereo chemical aspects of organic compounds and stereo chemical reactions
know the medicinal uses and other applications of organic compounds
courseOutcomes: CO1: Discuss the different types of isomerism and explaining the stereochemical reactions in organic compounds with their importance
CO2: Outline methods for the determination of the configuration of geometric isomers and discussing stereoselective and stereospecific reactions in organic molecules
CO3: Sketch about important heterocyclic compounds with a 5-membered and 6-membered ring system and explaining the reactions and aromaticity in 5-membered heterocyclic ring compounds
CO4: Explain the method of preparation and reactions of some important fused heterocyclics and outlining the Medicinal uses and applications of some important derivatives in heterocyclics
CO5: Sketch different organic reactions in the organic compounds and discussing the vital application of reagents in organic reactions

scope: This subject is designed to impart fundamental knowledge on the structure, chemistry and therapeutic value of drugs. The subject emphasizes on structure activity relationships of drugs, importance of physicochemical properties and metabolism of drugs. The syllabus also emphasizes on chemical synthesis of important drugs under each class.
objectives: understand the chemistry of drugs with respect to their pharmacological activity
understand the drug metabolic pathways, adverse effect and therapeutic value of drugs
know the Structural Activity Relationship (SAR) of different class of drugs
write the chemical synthesis of some drugs
courseOutcomes: CO1: Outline the history and development of medicinal chemistry and summarizing the physicochemical properties in relation to pharmacological activity, Explain the drug metabolisms process Phase I and Phase II.
CO2: Outline the biosynthesis and catabolism of catecholamines and explain the Structure, uses, Mechanism of action, synthesis and SAR of drugs acting on autonomic nervous system
CO3: Outline the biosynthesis and metabolism of acetylcholine and explain the Structure, MOA, uses, important synthesis and SAR of drugs acting on Cholinergic nervous system.
CO4: Illustrate the Structure, uses, synthesis, MOA and SAR of barbiturates, Benzodiazepines and phenothiazines derivatives, Discussing the SAR, MOA and synthesis of anticonvulsants
CO5: Discuss the Structure and synthesis of general anaesthetics. Identify the MOA, SAR and synthesis of narcotic and non-narcotic analgesics.

scope: Preparation of drugs/intermediates (1,3-pyrazole, 1,3-oxazole, Benzimidazole, Benztriazole, 2,3-diphenyl quinoxaline, Benzocaine, Phenytoin, Phenothiazine, Barbiturate), Assay of drugs, and determination of Partition coefficient.

Know the assay of Aspirin and Ibuprofen tablets.
Examine the percentage purity of furosemide tablets and determine the partition coefficient of drugs
Understand the synthesis of medicinal compounds like Phenytoin and Benzocaine.
Understand the synthesis of medicinal compounds like 2,3-diphenyl quinoxaline and 4-Benzylidene-2-phenyl oxazol-5-one.

scope: The course deals with the various physical, physicochemical properties and principle involved in dosage forms, formulations. Theory and practical components of the subject help the student to get a better insight in to various areas of formulation research and development and stability studies of pharmaceuticals.
objectives: Understand various physicochemical properties of drug molecules in the designing the dosage form
Know the principles of chemical kinetics and to use them in assigning expiry date for Formulation
Demonstrate use of physicochemical properties in evaluation of dosage forms.
Appreciate physicochemical properties of drug molecules in formulation research and Development
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain the classification of dispersed systems, general characteristics of colloidal particles and their general properties.
CO2: Summarise the various theories of Rheological systems, laws, equations related to different Rheological systems with use of deformation of solids
CO3: Illustrate the knowledge of disperse system, classification, theory of emulsification, HLB value in addition to formulation and evaluation of emulsions.
CO4: Evaluate particle size analysis and surface area determination methods.
CO5: Distinguish the principles of chemical kinetics, stability testing and determination of expiry date of formulations.

topics.topicName: Colloidal dispersions: Classification of dispersed systems and their general characteristics, size and shapes of colloidal particles, classification of colloids and comparative account of their general properties. Optical, kinetic and electrical properties. Effect of electrolytes, coacervation, peptization and protective action.
topics.name: Colloidal dispersions: Classification of dispersed systems and their general characteristics, size and shapes of colloidal particles, classification of colloids and comparative account of their general properties. Optical, kinetic and electrical properties. Effect of electrolytes, coacervation, peptization and protective action.

topics.topicName: Rheology: Newtonian systems, law of flow, kinematic viscosity, effect of temperature, non-Newtonian systems, pseudoplastic, dilatants, plastic, thixotropy, thixotropy in formulation, determination of viscosity, capillary, falling Sphere, rotational viscometers

topics.name: Deformation of solids: Plastic and elastic deformation, Heckel equation, Stress, Strain, Elastic Modulus.

topics.topicName: Coarse dispersion: Suspension, interfacial properties of suspended particles, settling in suspensions, formulation of flocculated and deflocculated suspensions.

topics.topicName: Emulsions and theories of emulsification, microemulsion and multiple emulsions; Stability of emulsions, preservation of emulsions, rheological properties of emulsions and emulsion formulation by HLB method.
topics.name: Emulsions and theories of emulsification, microemulsion and multiple emulsions; Stability of emulsions, preservation of emulsions, rheological properties of emulsions and emulsion formulation by HLB method.

topics.topicName: Micromeretics: Particle size and distribution, mean particle size, number and weight distribution, particle number, methods for determining particle size by different methods, counting and separation method, particle shape, specific surface, methods for determining surface area, permeability, adsorption, derived properties of powders, porosity, packing arrangement, densities, bulkiness and flow properties.
topics.name: Micromeretics: Particle size and distribution, mean particle size, number and weight distribution, particle number, methods for determining particle size by different methods, counting and separation method, particle shape, specific surface, methods for determining surface area, permeability, adsorption, derived properties of powders, porosity, packing arrangement, densities, bulkiness and flow properties.

topics.topicName: Drug stability: Reaction kinetics: zero, pseudo-zero, first and second order, units of basic rate constants, determination of reaction order. Physical and chemical factors influencing the chemical degradation of pharmaceutical product: temperature, solvent, ionic strength, dielectric constant, specific and general acid base catalysis, Simple numerical problems. Stabilization of medicinal agents against common reactions like hydrolysis and oxidation. Accelerated stability testing in expiration dating of pharmaceutical dosage forms. Photolytic degradation and its prevention.
topics.name: Drug stability: Reaction kinetics: zero, pseudo-zero, first and second order, units of basic rate constants, determination of reaction order. Physical and chemical factors influencing the chemical degradation of pharmaceutical product: temperature, solvent, ionic strength, dielectric constant, specific and general acid base catalysis, Simple numerical problems. Stabilization of medicinal agents against common reactions like hydrolysis and oxidation. Accelerated stability testing in expiration dating of pharmaceutical dosage forms. scope: Practical experiments for determining particle size, bulk density, angle of repose, viscosity, sedimentation volume, reaction rate constants, and stability studies.
objectives: Understand various physiochemical properties of drug molecules in evaluation of the dosage form
Know the principles of Chemical kinetics and to use them in assigning expiry date for formulation
Demonstrate the use of physiochemical properties in evaluation of dosage form
Appreciate physiochemical properties of drug molecules in Formulation, Research and Development
courseOutcomes: CO1: Evaluate the various physicochemical properties of drug and excipient molecules in designing the dosage forms.
CO2: Distinguish the principles of Physicochemical properties of drug and excipient molecules in designing the dosage forms.
CO3: Demonstrate use of physicochemical chemical kinetics & to use them for stability testing and determination of expiry date of formulations
CO4: Understand physicochemical properties of drug molecules in formulation research and development.
CO5: Determine the physicochemical properties of drug molecules in formulation research.

scope: The main purpose of the subject is to understand what drugs do to the living organisms and how their effects can be applied to therapeutics. The subject covers the information about the drugs like, mechanism of action, physiological and biochemical effects (pharmacodynamics) as well as absorption, distribution, metabolism and excretion (pharmacokinetics) along with the adverse effects, Clinical uses, interactions, doses, contraindications and routes of administration of different classes of drugs.
objectives: Understand the pharmacological actions of different categories of drugs
Explain the mechanism of drug action at organ system/sub cellular/macromolecular levels.
Apply the basic pharmacological knowledge in the prevention and treatment of various diseases.
Observe the effect of drugs on animals by simulated experiments
Appreciate correlation of pharmacology with other bio medical sciences
courseOutcomes: CO1: Explain the scope of general pharmacology, as well as the various routes of drug administration and the concept of pharmacokinetics.
CO2: Analyze the fundamentals of pharmacodynamics, the mechanism of action, types of receptors factors that influence drug action and drug interactions.
CO3: Investigate neurohumoral transmission in PNS and the classification of neurotransmitters and drugs that act on the parasympathetic and sympathetic nervous system.
CO4: Investigate neurohumoral transmission in the ANS, with a focus on the pharmacology of CNS acting drugs.
CO5: Describe the mechanism of action, pharmacological action, side effects, and therapeutic applications of Psychopharmacological drugs

topics.topicName: a. Introduction to Pharmacology- Definition, historical landmarks and scope of pharmacology, nature and source of drugs, essential drugs concept and routes of drug administration, Agonists, antagonists( competitive and non competitive), spare receptors, addiction, tolerance, dependence, tachyphylaxis, idiosyncrasy, allergy.
topics.name: a. Introduction to Pharmacology- Definition, historical landmarks and scope of pharmacology, nature and source of drugs, essential drugs concept and routes of drug administration, Agonists, antagonists( competitive and non competitive), spare receptors, addiction, tolerance, dependence, tachyphylaxis, idiosyncrasy, allergy.

topics.topicName: b. Pharmacokinetics- Membrane transport, absorption, distribution, metabolism and excretion of drugs. Enzyme induction, enzyme inhibition, kinetics of elimination
topics.name: b. Pharmacokinetics- Membrane transport, absorption, distribution, metabolism and excretion of drugs. Enzyme induction, enzyme inhibition, kinetics of elimination

topics.topicName: a. Pharmacodynamics- Principles and mechanisms of drug action. Receptor theories and classification of receptors, regulation of receptors. drug receptors interactions signal transduction mechanisms, G-protein–coupled receptors, ion channel receptor, transmembrane enzyme linked receptors, transmembrane JAK-STAT binding receptor and receptors that regulate transcription factors, dose response relationship, therapeutic index, combined effects of drugs and factors modifying drug action.
topics.name: a. Pharmacodynamics- Principles and mechanisms of drug action. Receptor theories and classification of receptors, regulation of receptors. drug receptors interactions signal transduction mechanisms, G-protein–coupled receptors, ion channel receptor, transmembrane enzyme linked receptors, transmembrane JAK-STAT binding receptor and receptors that regulate transcription factors, dose response relationship, therapeutic index, combined effects of drugs and factors modifying drug action.

topics.topicName: b. Adverse drug reactions. c. Drug interactions (pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic)
topics.name: b. Adverse drug reactions. c. Drug interactions (pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic)

topics.topicName: d. Drug discovery and clinical evaluation of new drugs -Drug discovery phase, preclinical evaluation phase, clinical trial phase, phases of clinical trials and pharmacovigilance.

scope: Practical exercises in experimental pharmacology, laboratory animals, routes of administration, blood withdrawal, anesthetics, skeletal muscle relaxants, anticonvulsant MES & PTZ screening, and rabbit eye experiments.
objectives: To demonstrate the maintenance of laboratory animals, blood collections techniques
Know about the routes of administration and evaluate the effect of drugs on rabbit eye
To evaluate the skeletal muscle relaxants, locomotor activity and anticatatonic activity.
To learn the screening methods for anxiolytic and local anesthetics
To demonstrate the ciliary motility of frog’s esophagus
courseOutcomes: CO1: Discuss about experimental pharmacology tools, and laboratory animals as well as the CPCSEA guidelines.
CO2: Demonstrate the common laboratory techniques like routes of administration, blood withdrawal, anesthetics and euthanasia used for animal studies.
CO3: Analyze the screening of skeletal muscle relaxants, locomotor activity and anticatatonic activity in laboratory animals
CO4: Evaluate the various screening models for anticonvulsant and anxiolytic activity.
CO5: Interpret the effects of various drugs on rabbit eye and demonstrating the ciliary motility of frog oesophagus.

scope: The subject involves the fundamentals of Pharmacognosy like scope, classification of crude drugs, their identification and evaluation, phytochemicals present in them and their medicinal properties.
objectives: to know the techniques in the cultivation and production of crude drugs
to know the crude drugs, their uses and chemical nature
know the evaluation techniques for the herbal drugs
to carry out the microscopic and morphological evaluation of crude drugs
courseOutcomes: CO1: Understand the introduction, history, scope, and development of Pharmacognosy and the classification systems of crude drugs.
CO2: Understand the quality control parameters, adulteration, and evaluation of drugs of natural origin using quantitative microscopy.
CO3: Understand the factors influencing the cultivation, collection, processing, and storage of natural drugs including plant hormones, polyploidy, and conservation.
CO4: Discuss plant tissue culture development, nutritional requirements, and applications in pharmacognosy including edible vaccines.
CO5: Classify and study primary metabolites (carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, lipids) and marine novel medicinal agents.

topics.topicName: Introduction to Pharmacognosy: (a) Definition, history, scope and development of Pharmacognosy. (b) Sources of Drugs – Plants, Animals, Marine and Tissue culture. (c) Organized drugs, unorganized drugs (dried latex, dried juices, dried extracts, gums and mucilages, oleoresins and oleo- gum -resins).
topics.name: Introduction to Pharmacognosy: (a) Definition, history, scope and development of Pharmacognosy. (b) Sources of Drugs – Plants, Animals, Marine and Tissue culture. (c) Organized drugs, unorganized drugs (dried latex, dried juices, dried extracts, gums and mucilages, oleoresins and oleo- gum -resins).

topics.topicName: Classification of drugs: Alphabetical, morphological, taxonomical, chemical, pharmacological, chemo and sero taxonomical classification of drugs
topics.name: Classification of drugs: Alphabetical, morphological, taxonomical, chemical, pharmacological, chemo and sero taxonomical classification of drugs

topics.topicName: Quality control of Drugs of Natural Origin: Adulteration of drugs of natural origin. Evaluation by organoleptic, microscopic, physical, chemical and biological methods and properties. Quantitative microscopy of crude drugs including lycopodium spore method, leaf constants, camera lucida and diagrams of microscopic objects to scale.
topics.name: Quality control of Drugs of Natural Origin: Adulteration of drugs of natural origin. Evaluation by organoleptic, microscopic, physical, chemical and biological methods and properties. Quantitative microscopy of crude drugs including lycopodium spore method, leaf constants, camera lucida and diagrams of microscopic objects to scale.

topics.topicName: Study of biological source, chemical nature and uses of drugs of natural origin containing following drugs. Plant Products: Fibers - Cotton, Jute, Hemp. Hallucinogens, Teratogens, Natural allergens.

topics.topicName: Primary metabolites: General introduction, detailed study with respect to chemistry, sources, preparation, evaluation, preservation, storage, therapeutic used and commercial utility as Pharmaceutical Aids and/or Medicines for the following Primary metabolites: Carbohydrates: Acacia, Agar, Tragacanth, Honey. Proteins and Enzymes : Gelatin, casein, proteolytic enzymes (Papain, bromelain, serratiopeptidase, urokinase, streptokinase, pepsin). Lipids(Waxes, fats, fixed oils) : Castor oil, Chaulmoogra oil, Wool Fat, Bees Wax.
topics.name: Primary metabolites: General introduction, detailed study with respect to chemistry, sources, preparation, evaluation, preservation, storage, therapeutic used and commercial utility as Pharmaceutical Aids and/or Medicines for the following Primary metabolites: Carbohydrates: Acacia, Agar, Tragacanth, Honey. Proteins and Enzymes : Gelatin, casein, proteolytic enzymes (Papain, bromelain, serratiopeptidase, urokinase, streptokinase, pepsin). Lipids(Waxes, fats, fixed oils) : Castor oil, Chaulmoogra oil, Wool Fat, Bees Wax.

scope: Practical exercises in analysis of crude drugs by chemical tests, determination of stomatal number/index, vein islet number/termination, paliside ratio, starch grain sizes, fiber length/width, Lycopodium spore method, ash value, extractive value, moisture content, swelling/foaming index.
objectives: to know the techniques in the cultivation and production of crude drugs
to know the crude drugs, their uses and chemical nature
know the evaluation techniques for the herbal drugs
to carry out the microscopic and morphological evaluation of crude drugs
courseOutcomes: CO1: Perform qualitative chemical tests for carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, and lipids on crude drugs.
CO2: Determine quantitative microscopy constants including stomatal index, vein-islet number, and palisade ratio.
CO3: Conduct microscopic measurements of starch grains and calcium oxalate crystals using eye-piece micrometer.
CO4: Determine physical evaluation constants of natural drugs including ash values, extractive values, moisture content, swelling and foaming index.
CO5: Conduct fiber measurements and Lycopodium spore quantitative evaluations.
